item.title: Zumba Toning *Online*
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 8:15 AM

Location: Online / Virtual

Tailored for active older adults\, who want to focus on muscle conditioning and light weight activity\, Zumba Gold Toning blends low-intensity strength training with a fun party atmosphere. Dress comfortably and bring water. Adults of all ages welcome. Start anytime. Please contact arielb@phinneycenter.org for link to attend this online-only class.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Cardio Sculpt Dance Fitness Class with Seattle Jazzercise
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 9:15 AM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Phinney Center\, Blue Building\, Room 7: Feel lighter\, stronger\, and happier after 40 minutes of high-intensity\, dance-based cardio followed by strength training and stretching. This is a group exercise class. Please bring a water bottle.  All other equipment will be provided. \n

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Easy Exercise
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Health benefits for all in this popular and low-key ongoing class. Gain strength and flexibility among friendly people. What a fun way to keep moving! All skill levels welcome. Taught by Gerry Joyce. Register for online: arielb@phinneycenter.org.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Hot Meal Program (lunch)
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: St John United Lutheran Church\, 5515 Phinney Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

In addition to providing a meal\, the Hot Meal Program provides a safe environment to feel cared for and to feel like a part of this neighborhood. We offer a variety of hygiene products\, gently-used clothing\, physical therapy and medical services as well. Volunteer opportunities are available.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Seattle Now & Then
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Wednesday\, April 8. 1-2pm. GSC. Free. \nNow & Then\, the photo history column in the Seattle Times\, has been running since 1982. Join columnists Jean Sherrard and Clay Eals as they share photos\, past columns\, behind-the-scenes stories\, and Seattle history. RSVP: 206-297-0875

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Cardio Sculpt Dance Fitness Class with Seattle Jazzercise
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 5:45 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Phinney Center\, Blue Building\, Room 7: Feel lighter\, stronger\, and happier after 40 minutes of high-intensity\, dance-based cardio followed by strength training and stretching. This is a group exercise class. Please bring a water bottle. All other equipment will be provided.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: PNA Woodshop\, dedicated to Bill Fenimore
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Tai Chi: Moving Meditation
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Come have fun! Build strength\, balance\, and agility with the centuries-old practices of Tai Chi and Qigong. Experience deep breathing\, slow flowing movement\, and meditation to maximize longevity and improve quality of life. Suitable for all ability levels. Taught by Larry Spangler. Register: 206.297.0875.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Line Dancing for All Levels
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 10:15 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

In this easy\, upbeat class\, you’ll explore music from Classic Rock to Latin\, Contemporary and — yes! — Western with 16\, 34 and 48 count routines. It’s great for anyone who enjoys moving to music. Exercise never felt like such fun! Benefits include increased flexibility\, brain enhancement\, better balance and stronger muscles. All levels are welcome. Taught by Chris Banta. Register: 206.297.0875.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Needle Arts Group
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Do you knit\, sew\, cross-stitch\, quilt\, or embroider? Rug hooking? Bargello? Any type of needle and thread/yarn work is welcome. Bring your project and enjoy some social interaction. Questions: 206.297.0875.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Cardio Sculpt Dance Fitness Class with Seattle Jazzercise
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Phinney Center\, Blue Building\, Room 7: Feel lighter\, stronger\, and happier after 40 minutes of high-intensity\, dance-based cardio followed by strength training and stretching. This is a group exercise class. Please bring a water bottle. All other equipment is provided.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Boutique Bellydancersize Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Explore the ancient & mysterious art of belly dancing\, with Boutique Bellydancersize classes! Specialized classes. Superior core workout. Strengthens & coordinates muscle groups in the trunk\, spine & arms. Improves posture & flexibility. Students will learn the basic steps\, undulations\, shimmies\, isolations\, tuxseem and more. All Ages. Group classes at Phinney Center. A focused gym that provides a sense of connection. Zaphara’s classes go beyond physical fitness\, including a broader sense of self care\, including sleep\, nutrition\, skin health\, stress management & socializing. Starts the 1st Thursday of the Month 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm – Beginners 7:45 pm – 9:00 pm Intermediate – Advanced 4 weeks: $80\, Drop-ins: $27 per class Taught by Zaphara\, Master Bellydance Instructor & Performer Sign up and pay online: bellydancingbyzaphara.com/classes

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Couth Buzzard Books — Thursday Open Mic
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 8310 Greenwood Ave N, Seattle

Acoustic, no-PA listening environment at an independent bookstore/café. All art forms welcome: music, poetry, dance, comedy, and stories.

From Couth Buzzard Books — Thursday Open Mic

item.title: AARP Tax Assistance
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Once you have compiled all the necessary paperwork to complete your return\, call 206.297.0875 to schedule an appointment with the AARP volunteer tax preparers. While this service is free (such a bargain!)\, donations to the GSC are appreciated.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Phinney/Greenwood Art Walk
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Phinney/Greenwood neighborhoods, Seattle

Monthly art walk in the Phinney/Greenwood neighborhoods

From Phinney/Greenwood Art Walk

Event details

item.title: CPR\, First Aid & HIV Certification
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Phinney Center Campus\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Learn first aid\, infant\, child and adult CPR\, HIV and bloodborne pathogens\, with a focus on injury prevention and safety. Meets L/I WISHA-WAC safety standards. Certificates issued at end of class. Can you provide first aid in emergencies\, locate and take care of injuries\, help a choking infant or child and stop cardiac arrest? Learn first aid\, infant\, child and adult CPR\, HIV and bloodborne pathogens\, with a focus on injury prevention and safety. Modified to meet COVID-19 safety requirements (hands-on only\, no mouth-to mouth). Meets L/I WISHA-WAC safety standards. Certificates issued at end of class. Taught by Ken Norris\, Retired Firefighter. Location: Upper Blue Building\, Room 7.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: PNA Woodshop\, dedicated to Bill Fenimore
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: The Canote Brothers in Concert
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Jere and Greg Canote are identical twins from Seattle\, WA who perform as the Canote Brothers. They are known nationally through their touring and teaching at music camps and festivals across the country. The brothers have played an important role in the old-time music scene on the West Coast through a string band class they have been teaching for several decades\, and they are known for their infectious\, positive energy\, and for humorously capitalizing on their relationship as identical twins. \nThey specialize in old time fiddle tunes\, saying “We love the hypnotic ‘in the moment’ experience of playing this American Old Time music.” From the synopsis of the recent documentary about them: “Anyone close enough to be caught up in this vortex of joy\, is likely to end up feeling happier and more optimistic about themselves and the world!” \nDoors open at 7 pm for the 7:30 pm concert in the Phinney Center’s Community Hall (lower Brick Building). \nTickets available online or at prior Seattle Folklore Society concerts\, or at the door (subject to availability).

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

Monday, April 13, 2026 at 8:15 AM

item.title: Easy Exercise
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Health benefits for all in this popular and low-key ongoing class. Gain strength and flexibility among friendly people. What a fun way to keep moving! All skill levels welcome. Taught by Gerry Joyce. Register for online: arielb@phinneycenter.org.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Pilates for Core & Balance
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Just under an hour of strength and balance training for standing and in-motion stability. This is a Pilates-based approach to strengthening\, stabilizing and building proprioceptive awareness for the core and lower body. We will use chairs\, bands and balls as we move (provided by the GSC). Just bring your body! Taught by Alethea Alexander. Register: 206.297.0875.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: National Scrabble Day Event
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Monday\, April 13\, 1-3pm. GSC. Free. \nJoin us for a celebration of this classic word game. All skill levels welcome. Expect snacks and good company. We’ll have boards and tiles\, but feel free to bring your own as well. RSVP: 206.297.0875. \n

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Intermediate/Advanced French
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Come and practice your French with French native speaker and experienced teacher Yasmina Mobarek. Relaxed atmosphere. Weekly on-going French conversation group. Open to Intermediate/Advanced speakers. \nRegister: 206.297.0875 or arielb@phinneycenter.org.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Hot Meal Program (dinner)
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: St John United Lutheran Church\, 5515 Phinney Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

In addition to providing a meal\, the Hot Meal Program provides a safe environment to feel cared for and to feel like a part of this neighborhood. We offer a variety of hygiene products\, gently-used clothing\, physical therapy and medical services as well. Volunteer opportunities are available.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Workshops
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Join us to learn the steps to home ownership and the resources that help pave the way! During part one\, we’ll cover the home buying process and best practices using recently sold homes as examples. Plus\, an informative Q&A with a home inspector. During part two\, we’ll dive deeper into available downpayment assistance (DPA) programs. You’re welcome to attend part one or both sessions. \nWe meet at 6pm every 2nd Monday of the month in the Phinney Center\, Blue Building. Snacks and NA drinks provided. Please RSVP at caitlin.lakere.com.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: PNA Woodshop\, dedicated to Bill Fenimore
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

item.title: Vital Sculpt HIIT Class with Seattle Jazzercise
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Phinney Center\, Blue Building\, Room 7: This invigorating HIIT-style exercise class promotes strength\, vitality\, and long-term hormone health with a science-backed blend of targeted sculpting\, high-intensity cardio\, and functional movement training. This is a group fitness class. Please bring a water bottle and an optional sweat towel. All other equipment will be provided. \nVital Sculpt HIIT meets every Tuesday at 5:30 pm at the Phinney Center.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details
